case_name: Michelle Tamayo v. Charles A. Debrah, M.D., an individual, Mercy Health Services - Iowa, Corporation d/b/a Mercy Medical Center - North Iowa and d/b/a Mercy Home Care - North, Iowa, Iowa Corporations

summary.brief: Michelle Tamayo v. Charles A. Debrah, M.D., an individual, Mercy Health Services - Iowa, Corporation d/b/a Mercy Medical Center - North Iowa and d/b/a Mercy Home Care - North, Iowa, Iowa Corporations. Medical malpractice case involving other.

evidence_index.text: Michelle Tamayo contracted a deep-tissue infection following surgery.
evidence_index.speaker: Court finding

evidence_index.text: Plaintiff appeals the district court’s ruling granting summary judgment in favor of defendants on a medical malpractice action. AFFIRMED.
evidence_index.speaker: Court finding

evidence_index.text: The district court (1) struck her expert witnesses for failure to timely designate them and (2) granted summary judgment in favor of the defendants.
evidence_index.speaker: Court finding

evidence_index.text: We conclude the district court did not abuse its discretion in striking the expert witnesses.
evidence_index.speaker: Court finding

evidence_index.text: We conclude the district court did not err in granting the defendants’ motion for summary judgment.
evidence_index.speaker: Court finding

evidence_index.text: Dr. Debrah stated 'in the process of doing any surgery, even though you scrub in and use sterile technique, there can be exposure to bacteria.'
evidence_index.speaker: Dr. Debrah

evidence_index.text: Tamayo concedes she failed to comply with the section 668.11 deadline and the agreed-upon extensions.
evidence_index.speaker: Court finding

evidence_index.text: The first factor—deviation from the deadline—was significant; Tamayo waited almost two months to designate her experts.
evidence_index.speaker: Court finding

evidence_index.text: The Hantsbarger factors do not support a finding of good cause for the late designation.
evidence_index.speaker: Court finding

evidence_index.text: Dr. Debrah made admissions establishing his negligence. See Oswald v. LeGrand, 453 N.W.2d 634, 640 (Iowa 1990).
evidence_index.speaker: Court finding

initial_state.history_of_present_illness: Patient developed a deep-tissue infection following surgery performed by Dr. Debrah.

focused_exam.system: Examination mentioned but findings not specified

requestables.name: Surgical consultation
requestables.description: Consultation with Dr. Debrah regarding post-surgical infection

reveal.result_summary: Discussion of incision through multiple layers of tissue and diagnosis of necrotizing fasciitis
reveal.clinical_significance: Highlights potential for infection and need for timely diagnosis
reveal.decision_impact: Should prompt consideration of necrotizing fasciitis

timeline_phases.name: Post-Surgical Presentation
timeline_phases.description: Patient presents with post-surgical pain and infection

decision_points.clinical_context: Patient with post-surgical pain and infection
decision_points.prompt: What should be the next step in managing the patient's post-surgical infection?

options.description: Order immediate imaging and consult infectious disease specialist
options.clinical_reasoning: Timely diagnosis and management of necrotizing fasciitis is critical

options.description: Monitor symptoms and provide standard post-operative care
options.clinical_reasoning: Failed to address the potential severity of necrotizing fasciitis

actual_action_defendant.description: Defendant monitored symptoms without immediate intervention
actual_action_defendant.reasoning_stated: Defendant stated post-surgical pain was not indicative of necrotizing fasciitis

expected_action_court.description: Immediate imaging and specialist consultation should have been ordered
expected_action_court.standard_of_care_basis: Expert evidence required for timely diagnosis of necrotizing fasciitis

explanation.why_defendant_wrong: Failure to timely diagnose necrotizing fasciitis led to worsened condition
explanation.what_should_have_happened: Immediate intervention could have prevented progression
explanation.legal_standard_applied: Standard of care as established by expert testimony

patient_outcome.description: Patient developed necrotizing fasciitis following surgery

key_findings: Summary judgment granted due to lack of expert testimony
No genuine issue of material fact on negligence or causation

malpractice_determination.point_of_failure: Failure to timely diagnose necrotizing fasciitis
malpractice_determination.counterfactual: Timely diagnosis could have prevented progression

allegations.allegation: Failure to timely diagnose necrotizing fasciitis

court_findings.finding: No genuine issue of material fact on negligence or causation
